utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format has 6 items: property, financing, legal_services, moving_services, setup_services, total_budget. The final solution includes all 6 items with entries. Now check if each is fully specified: (1) property: includes type, beds/baths, style, location, features, price, status — sufficient. However, it only lists one property while user asked to find suitable 4+ bedroom homes; but the format only requires a selected property with details and price, which is present. (2) financing: includes mortgage_amount, interest_rate, type, status, and down_payment_source — terms like amortization period and monthly payment are missing, but the format only says 'financing solution with terms.' It does include an interest rate and type; arguably minimal terms are present, though not comprehensive; still qualifies as included with terms. (3) legal_services: provider, service, cost, status — complete. (4) moving_services: provider, service, cost, status — complete. (5) setup_services: utilities listed, cost, property_survey with cost and schedule, status — complete. (6) total_budget: cost breakdown lines and totals, budget limit, remaining — complete. Therefore, all 6/6 items from the format are present and reasonably specified.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final solution includes specific selections that correspond to items in the ground-truth ratings: property = Historic Townhouse; financing = Traditional Mortgage; legal_services = Premium Legal Package; moving_services = Standard Moving (matches price CAD $650 and description); setup_services utilities = Premium Utilities; property_survey = Full Structural Survey. I mapped each chosen item to its exact rating from the ground-truth list.
